name: Percy
description: A modular toolkit for building isomorphic web apps with Rust + WebAssembly, featuring virtual DOM and SSR support

description.value: A modular toolkit for building isomorphic web apps with Rust + WebAssembly, featuring virtual DOM and SSR support

initial-release.comment: First GitHub issue created July 26, 2018; creator started using Rust in January 2018

latest-release.comment: percy-router 0.5.1 released August 23, 2024; percy-dom 0.10.0 released approximately December 2023

current-version.comment: percy-dom 0.10.0 is the core crate; no formal GitHub releases tagged

active-maintenance.comment: Development has slowed significantly; described as going on 'life support' compared to active alternatives like Yew and Leptos

reactivity-model.comment: Uses virtual DOM with diff/patch algorithm; creates virtual representation, diffs, and patches real DOM

component-model.comment: Components defined as Rust functions returning VirtualNode via html! macro

rendering-paradigm.comment: Declarative html! macro generates virtual DOM that can render to DOM or String

state-management-approach.comment: percy-state crate available for application state management; not required for basic usage

opinionatedness.comment: Modular toolkit approach; can use components independently

backward-compatibility.comment: Pre-1.0 software; explicitly stated as 'not yet ready for production'

primary-language.comment: Written entirely in Rust (100% according to GitHub)

compiles-to.comment: Compiles to WebAssembly for browser; also compiles to native for server-side rendering

typescript-support.comment: Rust framework; not applicable - uses Rust's type system

template-syntax.comment: html! macro for generating virtual DOM; supports interpolation, events, classes, and conditional rendering

styling-approach.comment: CSS-in-Rust via percy-css-macro; allows writing styles next to html! components

build-required.comment: Requires Rust toolchain with wasm32-unknown-unknown target; uses wasm-bindgen

wasm-interop.comment: Uses wasm-bindgen and web-sys for JavaScript interop; DOM API calls noted as performance bottleneck

reactive-primitives.comment: No built-in reactive primitives like signals; uses virtual DOM diffing approach

computed-values.comment: No built-in computed/derived values; relies on Rust's standard patterns

two-way-binding.comment: No built-in two-way binding; events and state managed explicitly

event-handling.comment: Declarative event binding in html! macro with closures

conditional-rendering.comment: Conditional rendering via Rust control flow within html! macro

list-rendering.comment: List rendering via Rust iterators within html! macro

component-slots.comment: Custom components can accept children as VirtualNode

suspense.comment: No built-in suspense or async loading boundaries

context-provide-inject.comment: No built-in context or dependency injection; percy-state provides app state management

refs-direct-dom-access.comment: Direct DOM access via web-sys bindings

official-router.comment: percy-router is the official first-party routing solution

nested-routes.comment: Not clearly documented in available sources

dynamic-routes.comment: Type-safe URL parameters mentioned in documentation

lazy-loading-routes.comment: Not documented; WASM binary splitting not explicitly mentioned

built-in-state.comment: No built-in reactive state; uses Rust patterns for local component state

built-in-global-state.comment: percy-state is a separate optional crate, not built into core

global-state-library.comment: Official percy-state crate for application state management (v0.1.2)

devtools-state-inspection.comment: No browser devtools extension; relies on standard WASM debugging

official-cli.comment: No dedicated CLI; uses standard Cargo tooling with wasm-pack or trunk

hot-module-replacement.comment: No built-in HMR; depends on build tooling used (trunk, wasm-pack)

browser-devtools.comment: No Percy-specific browser devtools; uses standard WASM debugging with console_error_panic_hook

vs-code-extension.comment: rust-analyzer provides Rust language support; no Percy-specific extension

error-messages.comment: Rust compiler provides excellent error messages; Percy-specific errors are basic but improving

documentation-quality.comment: The Percy Book is incomplete ('work in progress, some chapters are empty placeholders'); API docs available on docs.rs

playground.comment: No online playground; requires Rust toolchain

bundle-size-core.comment: percy-dom crate is ~38.9 KiB; actual WASM output varies significantly by application and optimization settings

bundle-size-full.comment: Estimated ~150 kB gzipped with router and state; WASM bundles can be optimized with wasm-opt

virtual-dom.comment: Core feature; generates patches to transform old VirtualNode into new VirtualNode

compile-time-optimization.comment: Rust compiler performs extensive optimizations; html! macro generates efficient code

tree-shaking.comment: Rust's dead code elimination; wasm-opt can further optimize WASM binaries

code-splitting.comment: No documented WASM binary splitting support

ssr-capable.comment: Core feature; virtual DOM renders to String on server for SSR with hydration

streaming-ssr.comment: SSR renders to complete String; no streaming support documented

partial-hydration.comment: Full hydration approach; no islands architecture

js-framework-benchmark.comment: Not included in js-framework-benchmark; interest expressed in GitHub issue #153 but not implemented

npm-weekly-downloads.comment: Rust crate, not npm package; distributed via crates.io (~47k total downloads for percy-dom)

component-libraries.comment: No dedicated UI component libraries for Percy

meta-framework.comment: Percy itself is designed for isomorphic apps; no separate meta-framework

major-companies-using.comment: No publicly listed production users; explicitly stated as not production-ready

official-sponsor-backer.comment: Personal project by Chinedu Francis Nwafili; community-driven development

discord-community-size.comment: No dedicated Percy Discord; community uses general Rust Discord channels

stack-overflow-questions.comment: Minimal Stack Overflow presence; no dedicated tag

job-market-demand.comment: Niche; experimental framework not widely adopted in production

official-testing-library.comment: Built-in testing utilities for unit testing views via virtual DOM inspection

testing-library-name.comment: Tests use children_recursive() and virtual node methods to verify rendering

component-testing.comment: Component testing via virtual DOM inspection; examples provided in repository

snapshot-testing.comment: Can snapshot test via virtual DOM String rendering

desktop-framework.comment: No official desktop solution; could work with Tauri but not documented

learning-curve.comment: Steep learning curve; requires Rust knowledge plus framework-specific concepts (virtual DOM, html! macro)

concepts-to-learn.comment: Rust ownership, wasm-bindgen, html! macro, virtual DOM, diffing/patching, SSR, routing, state management

time-to-hello-world.comment: ~15 minutes with Rust toolchain installed; requires wasm-pack or trunk setup

migration-path.comment: No migration guides from other frameworks

incremental-adoption.comment: Cannot be incrementally added to existing JS applications; requires full Rust toolchain
